title: Zeroizing<[u8;32]> for OrchardSpendingKey: correct and must not be changed to plain bytes
description: orchard.rs stores the spending key bytes in Zeroizing<[u8; 32]> which overwrites memory on drop. A contributor simplifying the struct might replace this with a plain [u8; 32], leaving key material in heap memory after the struct is dropped where it could be swapped to disk or captured in a core dump. The Zeroizing wrapper is a security property, not defensive paranoia. The no-Debug impl on OrchardSpendingKey is also correct — spending key material must never appear in tracing output.
